summary: Eight concrete products and tools surfaced from HN comments, with strong signals in local AI, microVMs, web presence layers, and retro hardware curiosities.

items.name: Mistral Vibe
items.summary: Mistral's AI chat and coding agent — a commenter said it already beats Opus at writing.
items.why_included: Concrete alternative the thread surfaced for post-Claude workflows.
items.evidence: A commenter said they installed Mistral Vibe, use it for about half their LLM tasks, and find it fast to iterate.

items.name: DwarfStar
items.source_story_title: Two Qwen3 models on one DGX Spark: the residency math
items.summary: DwarfStar is a local DeepSeek V4 Flash/PRO inference engine — a commenter said it was enough to move from one DGX Spark to two.
items.why_included: Clear praise for a concrete local-AI tool in a hardware-constrained thread.
items.evidence: A commenter said they started with DwarfStar on one Spark, then jumped to two Sparks, and were excited about the future of local LLMs.

items.name: smolvm
items.source_story_title: Running MicroVMs in Proxmox VE, the Easy Way
items.summary: smolvm is a lightweight microVM tool built around libkrun — a commenter called it a favorite discovery.
items.why_included: Strong comment-backed recommendation from the microVM discussion.
items.evidence: A commenter said their favorite recent microVM discovery is tools built around libkrun and pointed to smolvm.

items.name: TownSquare
items.source_story_title: Show HN: TownSquare, a tiny presence layer for websites
items.summary: TownSquare is a tiny presence layer for websites — commenters liked the ambient social idea despite moderation pain.
items.why_included: Show HN with strong positive interest and repeated intent to use it.
items.evidence: Comments called it cute, cool, clever, and said they would consider adding it to their sites, even as moderation became the big concern.

items.name: floppinux
items.source_story_title: An Embedded Linux on a Single Floppy
items.summary: floppinux is an embedded Linux-on-a-floppy project — commenters marveled at the 1.44MB web-server feat.
items.why_included: A concrete technical stunt that drew nostalgia and respect.
items.evidence: Comments highlighted a full network stack and web server on a 1.44MB floppy and compared it with older rescue-floppy distros.

items.name: Beyond All Reason
items.source_story_title: Beyond All Reason (Free Total Annihilation Inspired RTS)
items.summary: Beyond All Reason is a free RTS inspired by Total Annihilation — commenters praised the game and its solo/co-op modes.
items.why_included: Large, clearly positive game thread with substantive discussion.
items.evidence: Replies praised the polish, single-player scenarios, skirmish mode, and co-op even as public multiplayer toxicity got debated.

items.name: Avoxelgame
items.source_story_title: A 3D voxel game engine written in APL
items.summary: Avoxelgame is a 3D voxel engine written in APL — commenters were fascinated by the language choice and honest README.
items.why_included: Novel project with direct praise and a clear canonical repo.
items.evidence: Commenters said the README does not oversell it, called the APL choice unusual, and asked about performance versus C++ or Rust.

items.name: Unihertz
items.source_story_title: The Commodore Callback 8020 smart flip phone
items.summary: Unihertz makes niche smartphones — commenters recommended the brand as a better alternative to the Commodore flip phone.
items.why_included: Concrete product recommendation surfaced directly from the phone thread.
items.evidence: One commenter said Unihertz phones are the only ones they have seen beat the alternative-phone price dynamic to an extent.
